levels.grammarNote: Adverb form: 快乐地 (kuàilè de - happily). In Chinese, add 地 (de) after an adjective to form an adverb. E.g., '她快乐地笑着。' (She was smiling happily.)
Adverb form: 悲伤地 (bēishāng de - sadly). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '他悲伤地点头。' (He nodded sadly.)

levels.back: 他的愤怒让大家都害怕。
levels.romanized: tā de fènnù ràng dàjiā dōu hàipà.
levels.literal: His anger make everyone all afraid.

levels.romanized: tā wúfǎ yǐncáng tā de kǒngjù.

levels.front: I hope you feel better soon.
levels.romanized: wǒ xīwàng nǐ zǎorì hǎo qǐlái.
levels.literal: I hope you early-day good come-up.

levels.front: He has a lot of stress at work.
I'm in a good mood today.

levels.romanized: wǒ yǒu yī zhǒng qíguài de gǎnjué.
levels.literal: I have one kind-of strange feeling.

levels.back: 音乐能激发深深的情感。
levels.romanized: yīnyuè néng jīfā shēnshēn de qínggǎn.

levels.back: 他的兴奋感染了所有人。
levels.romanized: tā de xīngfèn gǎnrǎn le suǒyǒu rén.

levels.grammarNote: Adverb form: 平静地 (píngjìng de - calmly). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '她平静地回答。' (She answered calmly.)

levels.front: She stayed calm in the chaos.
levels.back: 她在混乱中保持了平静。
levels.romanized: tā zài hùnluàn zhōng bǎochí le píngjìng.
levels.literal: She in chaos middle maintained [now] calm.

levels.front: She has a desire to travel.
It's a pleasure to meet you.
She is in a lot of pain.
I don't have much energy today.
I felt great relief after the test.
levels.back: 考试后我感到非常宽慰。
levels.romanized: kǎoshì hòu wǒ gǎndào fēicháng kuānwèi.
levels.literal: Exam after I felt extremely relieved.

levels.front: She needed comfort after the bad news.
levels.back: 听到坏消息后她需要安慰。
levels.romanized: tīngdào huài xiāoxi hòu tā xūyào ānwèi.
levels.literal: Hear bad news after she needed comfort.

levels.front: I enjoy spending time with friends.
levels.back: 我享受与朋友共度时光。
levels.romanized: wǒ xiǎngshòu yǔ péngyou gòngdù shíguāng.
levels.literal: I enjoy with friends together-spend time.

levels.romanized: yīnyuè néng shǐ nǐ xīnqíng píngjìng.
levels.literal: Music can cause you mood calm.

levels.back: 这个消息使大家都很兴奋。
levels.romanized: zhège xiāoxi shǐ dàjiā dōu hěn xīngfèn.
levels.literal: This news cause everyone all very excited.

levels.back: 这个进球使观众非常激动。
levels.romanized: zhège jìnqiú shǐ guānzhòng fēicháng jīdòng.
levels.literal: This goal cause audience extremely thrilled.

levels.romanized: tā de xíngwéi jīnù le tā.

levels.back: 不公平的待遇使他愤怒。
levels.romanized: bù gōngpíng de dàiyù shǐ tā fènnù.
zhè fèn lǐwù shǐ tā hěn gāoxìng.
levels.literal: This gift make her very happy.

levels.romanized: tā shēnshēn de tàn le kǒu qì.

levels.back: 她对这个问题皱起了眉头。
levels.romanized: tā duì zhège wèntí zhòu qǐ le méitóu.
levels.literal: She toward this question wrinkled up [now] brow.

levels.front: She screamed when she saw the spider.
levels.back: 她看到蜘蛛时尖叫起来。
levels.romanized: tā kàndào zhīzhū shí jiānjiào qǐlái.
levels.literal: She see spider when screamed up-come.

levels.romanized: bù yào duì wǒ dà hǎn.

levels.grammarNote: Adverb form: 生气地 / 愤怒地 (shēngqì de / fènnù de - angrily). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '他生气地走了。' (He left angrily.)

levels.romanized: tā yīn yālì ér fānù le.

levels.grammarNote: Adverb form: 焦虑地 (jiāolǜ de - anxiously). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '她焦虑地等待着。' (She waited anxiously.)

levels.front: She felt anxious before the exam.
levels.romanized: kǎoshì qián tā gǎndào hěn jiāolǜ.
levels.literal: Exam before she felt very anxious.

levels.grammarNote: Adverb form: 紧张地 (jǐnzhāng de - nervously). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '他紧张地说话。' (He spoke nervously.)
Adverb form: 兴奋地 (xīngfèn de - excitedly). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '她兴奋地叫了起来。' (She cried out excitedly.)

levels.front: The children are excited about the trip.
levels.back: 孩子们对这次旅行很兴奋。
levels.romanized: háizimen duì zhè cì lǚxíng hěn xīngfèn.
levels.literal: Children toward this trip very excited.

levels.front: He is content with his life.
levels.back: 他对自己的生活很满足。
levels.romanized: tā duì zìjǐ de shēnghuó hěn mǎnzú.
levels.literal: He toward his-own life very content.

levels.front: I feel very stressed this week.
levels.romanized: zhè zhōu wǒ gǎndào yālì hěn dà.
levels.literal: This week I felt stress very big.

levels.front: She felt overwhelmed by the workload.
levels.back: 她被工作量压得不堪重负。
levels.romanized: tā bèi gōngzuòliàng yā de bùkān zhòngfù.
levels.literal: She by workload pressed to unable-to-bear heavy-burden.

levels.romanized: tā xǐng lái gǎndào jīnglì chōngpèi.
levels.literal: He wake come felt energy overflowing.

levels.grammarNote: Adverb form: 疲惫地 (píbèi de - wearily). Add 地 after the adjective. E.g., '他疲惫地拖着脚步。' (He dragged his feet wearily.)

levels.front: After the race, he was exhausted.
levels.back: 比赛结束后，他筋疲力尽。
levels.romanized: bǐsài jiéshù hòu, tā jīnpí lìjìn.
levels.literal: Race finish after, he muscles-tired strength-exhausted.

levels.romanized: tā duì yīqiè dōu hěn hàoqí.
levels.literal: She toward everything all very curious.
